outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum expires tonight; Iran defiant as IDF strikes Tehran overnight
outlets.summary: CNN's Day 24 live coverage leads with Trump's expiring Hormuz ultimatum and Iran's refusal to comply, while reporting on overnight IDF strikes on Tehran ballistic missile sites.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Iran refuses to give in, seeking greater global economic pain
outlets.summary: WaPo analysis focuses on Iran's strategy of leveraging Hormuz to inflict maximum global economic pain, and its refusal to bow to Trump's demands despite devastating strikes.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Day 23: Iran responds to Trump's power plant threat with complete Hormuz closure warning
outlets.summary: CNN's live coverage documents Day 23's major escalation: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum, Iran's counter-threat to fully close Hormuz and strike Gulf energy infrastructure, and overnight IDF strikes on Tehran.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Trump threatens Iran with strikes on power plants over Strait of Hormuz blockade
outlets.summary: Bloomberg reports Trump's ultimatum and notes Saudi Arabia found a workaround via the East-West Pipeline to Yanbu Red Sea port, which became operational as an alternate oil route.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump gives Iran final Hormuz warning as IDF destroys more missile sites; clock ticking on power plant strikes
outlets.summary: Fox News frames the Hormuz ultimatum as Trump asserting strength, with supportive coverage of overnight IDF strikes and the 22-nation coalition forming to patrol the strait.

outlets.name: Wall Street Journal
outlets.headline: Trump Presses Iran as 48-Hour Hormuz Ultimatum Draws Global Response
outlets.summary: WSJ analysis of the strategic calculus: 22 NATO allies mobilizing, Saudi Arabia activating bypass pipeline, Iran threatening Gulf-wide energy infrastructure strikes if power plants hit.

outlets.name: New York Post
outlets.headline: Trump to Pope: 'I don't want a ceasefire' — rejects Vatican peace call as IDF destroys Iran missile sites
outlets.summary: NY Post highlights Trump's blunt rejection of Pope Leo XIV's Angelus ceasefire appeal and overnight IDF strikes destroying IRGC ballistic missile production facilities in Tehran.

outlets.name: CBS News
outlets.headline: CENTCOM: US has struck 8,000+ Iranian targets; 130 naval vessels destroyed since war began
outlets.summary: CBS News live updates report CENTCOM Commander Admiral Cooper's announcement of an 8,000+ target milestone and 130 Iranian naval vessels destroyed — called 'largest navy elimination since WWII.'

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war Day 24: IDF demolishes Lebanon bridges, overnight Tehran strikes as Hormuz deadline looms
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera leads with IDF demolition of all Litani River bridges, overnight strikes on Tehran, and Lebanon's President calling bridge demolitions a "prelude to ground invasion."

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Trump and Iran trade war threats with Hormuz crisis building to Monday deadline
outlets.summary: Reuters focuses on the diplomatic standoff: Iran's IRGC doctrine shift to 'offensive posture,' 22 NATO nations mobilizing, Spain's PM demanding Hormuz opening, and the IEA's calls for emergency reserves.

outlets.name: PBS NewsHour
outlets.headline: Iran threatens to completely close Strait of Hormuz following Trump's 48-hour ultimatum
outlets.summary: PBS documents Iran's escalating counter-threats: full Hormuz closure, strikes on Gulf desalination plants, and targeting of US/Israeli IT infrastructure including Google, Microsoft, and Palantir offices.

outlets.name: NBC News
outlets.headline: Iran unswayed by Trump's 48-hour deadline and threats to 'obliterate' energy infrastructure
outlets.summary: NBC analysis of why Iran is holding firm: domestic nationalist pressure, regime survival calculus, IRGC's offensive doctrine shift, and Araghchi's messaging to international audience about insurance fears.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Day 24: IDF destroys 4 IRGC missile sites overnight; Nahariya hit; Hormuz ultimatum expires tonight
outlets.summary: Times of Israel covers overnight strikes on Tehran ballistic missile facilities, Nahariya rocket attack (6 wounded), Ben Gurion Airport restrictions, and the approaching Hormuz ultimatum deadline.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Netanyahu at Arad warns Europe to join war on Iran: 'It's time for them to act'
outlets.summary: Jerusalem Post reports Netanyahu's Arad speech invoking Trump's call for a global coalition against Iran and his warning that Iran's actions endanger all of civilization.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: In Israel's Second Iran War, Life Is Cheap and Caring About the Dead Is a Sin
outlets.summary: Haaretz op-ed takes a critical view of the war's civilian cost, arguing Israel's war culture has normalized Iranian civilian casualties and suppressed mourning for Israeli dead.

outlets.name: Ynet News
outlets.headline: Ben Gurion Airport restricts flights to 1 per hour, 50 passengers max starting Monday
outlets.summary: Ynet reports Israel's Transportation Ministry restricting Ben Gurion Airport operations starting Monday, effective response to ongoing Iranian missile threat and direct hits on southern Israel.

outlets.name: PressTV
outlets.headline: Islamic Republic resists US-Zionist aggression; IRGC warns: strike our power, we strike yours
outlets.summary: Iranian state media frames the conflict as resistance against "US-Zionist aggression," highlighting IRGC's counter-threat on Gulf infrastructure and Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf's warning about irreversible damage to desalination facilities.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Araghchi tells UN Security Council: Power plant strikes are 'inherently disproportionate'; demands Israel's nuclear sites be monitored
outlets.summary: IRNA covers Iran's formal UN letter condemning US-Israeli strikes as violations of international law, demanding reparations, and calling for IAEA monitoring of Israeli nuclear facilities.

outlets.name: Tasnim News
outlets.headline: IRGC releases target list of US tech firms in Gulf: Google, Microsoft, Palantir, IBM, Nvidia, Oracle
outlets.summary: Tasnim (IRGC-affiliated) published a target list of US tech company offices in Israel and Gulf states as part of threatened 'infrastructure war' if power plants are attacked — signaling expanded cyber-physical retaliation plans.

outlets.name: Fars News
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian: Hormuz open to all except those who violate Iranian soil; calls on BRICS to act
outlets.summary: Fars News covers President Pezeshkian's rebuttal to Trump's ultimatum, calling the Hormuz blockade a shipping insurance crisis caused by US aggression, and urging BRICS nations to play an independent role.
